story.text: As bdo, I need my viewport to be the whole tree, not just a window onto the landed trunk — a decorated, in-repo, auto-managed read of the whole forest of in-flight work (worktrees, branches, parked atoms, open PRs) — so I can see what is happening across the fleet and my physical checkout stops being a blocker, because I said 'I want my viewport to be the whole tree ... a decorated/tagged/flagged instance of the work, in repo, managed automatically ... I don't want MY viewport to be a blocker anymore.'

briefing.headline: The whole-tree forest fold — the decorated read over the whole forest
briefing.value: Your viewport shows only landed work; the forest of in-flight work is invisible from it and your checkout is load-bearing. This is the v0 read: loop/forest.py is a pure read-only fold that composes the git forest (worktrees + branches + per-branch PR state, the way the git pen's garden senses it) with each atom's pipeline state (reconcile.Fold) into ONE decorated FOREST.md — every work-item flagged parked-atom / stranded / live-worktree / in-review / merged — plus a --json twin.
briefing.why_now: The forest is already large (dozens of worktrees, scores of branches, parked atoms) and none of it is visible from the trunk-pinned checkout. Without a folded read, the only way to see in-flight work is to walk git by hand, and a dirty or stranded primary tree blocks the one surface you have. The fold makes the checkout one leaf among many.
briefing.if_accepted: FOREST.md becomes the decorated whole-tree read, regenerable by one command; v1 adds live sessions/environments via watcher.py as a source; the auto-regen hook/tick and the served HTML view ride later pieces; the surface becomes your real reading surface so the checkout stops being load-bearing.
briefing.if_rejected: The viewport stays a window onto the trunk only; in-flight work stays invisible from it and the physical checkout stays a blocker (the :whiteout utensil keeps treating the symptom).
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: forest.py is a pure read-only fold that composes existing folds (garden's sensing, reconcile.Fold, the digest's atoms_on_main) and writes only the generated FOREST.md on --write — reverting is deleting one module and one generated file. No truth is moved, no new write path, nothing pruned.
briefing.mechanism: loop/forest.py senses worktrees (git worktree list --porcelain), PR states (gh pr list --state all), per-worktree uncommitted (git status --porcelain) and loose branches the same way git.py garden does, folds each atom's state via reconcile.Fold + orchestrate.next_action + digest.atoms_on_main, and decorates every node with one closed-vocabulary status through two pure classifiers carrying the §10 teeth. 'render' emits FOREST.md, '--json' the dataset, '--write' regenerates the file; default renders to stdout and writes nothing.

source_artifacts: bdo 2026-06-22: 'I want my viewport to be the whole tree ... a decorated/tagged/flagged instance of the work, in repo, managed automatically ... I don't want MY viewport to be a blocker anymore.'
